SEARCH
Package compiler.ast

Class Rule

java.lang.Object
compiler.ast.ASTNode
compiler.ast.Rule
All Implemented Interfaces:
Rule
public class Rule extends ASTNode implements Rule
Represents a single rule in the program. A rule is a reactive unit: if its condition is true, its command executes. Rules are the fundamental building block of critter behavior. The program is simply a sequence of rules, typically evaluated in order until one matches. The condition and command are stored together as a single logical unit, making it easy for later phases (analysis, interpretation, code generation) to reason about rules as complete decision units without decomposing them.
See Also:
  • Condition
  • Command
  • Program

  • Constructor Details

    • Rule

      public Rule(Condition condition, Command command, int line, int column)
      Creates a new rule pairing a condition and command. The condition and command are evaluated as a unit: the rule only fires if the condition is true. Position information is preserved from the first token of the rule for accurate error reporting.
      Parameters:
      condition - the guard condition (non-null)
      command - the command to execute (non-null)
      line - source line where the rule starts
      column - source column where the rule starts

  • Method Details

    • getCondition

      public Condition getCondition()
      Returns the condition that must be true for this rule to fire.
      Returns:
      the Condition node (never null)

    • getCommand

      public Command getCommand()
      Returns the command to execute if this rule's condition is true.
      Returns:
      the Command node (never null)

    • getChildren

      public List<ASTNode> getChildren()
      Description copied from class: ASTNode
      Returns the immediate children of this node in source order. This method is used by tree traversal algorithms (printing, analysis, transforms) to navigate the AST structure. The order of children mirrors their appearance in source code.
      Specified by:
      getChildren in class ASTNode
      Returns:
      list of direct child nodes (may be empty, never null)

    • accept

      public <T> T accept(ASTVisitor<T> visitor)
      Description copied from class: ASTNode
      Accepts a visitor to perform an operation on this node. This implements the visitor design pattern, allowing new operations to be added without modifying the AST node classes. The visitor pattern enables clean separation between tree structure and algorithms (printing, analysis, code generation, etc.).
      Specified by:
      accept in class ASTNode
      Type Parameters:
      T - the return type produced by the visitor
      Parameters:
      visitor - the visitor to apply (non-null)
      Returns:
      the result produced by the visitor's visit method for this node type
